TextENGNew: Plans for Satoko-chan's dissection were moving steadily forward.
I didn't want to make Satoshi-kun sad.
I felt genuine sympathy for Satoko-chan and the harshness of her circumstances.
...And the obligation to carry out my merciless duty for the sake of this research—these feelings warred endlessly within me.
"Huh?@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1007. Ah, I'm sorry, I was lost in thought.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1008. What is it, Rika-chan?"
changeReason: New translation adds 'I was lost in thought' for うっかりしていました which was omitted in the original, providing context for Irie's distraction.

TextENGNew: "......A friend of mine has been hospitalized here for a long time. Does she have Hinamizawa Syndrome?"
"Who told you that?"
"......No one. I figured it out on my own.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1004. So what is it, Irie?@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1005. Does Satoko have Hinamizawa Syndrome?"
She was already one of us—someone on our side of the curtain.
There was nothing I needed to hide from her.
In fact, I had always believed it was my unwritten rule of integrity never to keep secrets from her.
changeReason: New translation captures the nuance of 誠意の不文律 (unwritten rule of integrity/sincerity) that was simplified in the original to just 'I probably shouldn't keep anything from her.'

TextENGNew: "......Yes.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1011. The problems with her parents, and then their sudden deaths...@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1012. It was far too much for her heart to absorb all at once.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1013. ...That may have been what triggered it."
"......What did the test results say?"

"L5.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1015. On the surface she appears quite stable, but emotionally she's extremely volatile.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1016. As of the other day, I've suspended Satoshi-kun's visits as well."
changeReason: Old attributes the decision to Satoko ('she's been refusing visits') but JPN 謝絶にしました indicates Irie made the decision to suspend Satoshi's visits.

TextENGNew: "......Satoshi told me.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1008. He's worried that it's taking so long for Satoko's wounds to heal.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1009. ...Irie.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1010. What will happen to Satoko?"
Satoko-chan was Rika-chan's closest friend.
How could I possibly tell her that preparations for Satoko-chan's dissection were already underway...?
But even if I said nothing, my silence would only confirm that Satoko-chan's situation was dire.
"......Didn't you learn a great deal by examining my body?"
"Of course we did...@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1019. We're even testing reagents that may suppress the symptoms."
changeReason: Old translation rendered 試薬 as 'medicine' but it specifically means 'reagent/experimental drug'; new translation uses the more precise term.

TextENGNew: "......Can't you give that medicine to Satoko?"

"I've considered it...@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1021. but it's far too dangerous.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1022. We're still in the stage of testing on subjects with sufficient physical and mental reserves, gathering data one step at a time.
...It's not something we can use on someone in as precarious a state as Satoko-chan."
"......Then, Irie.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1014. If you won't try that medicine on her, what will become of Satoko?"
She spoke as though she already knew the fate that awaited Satoko-chan.
...No—perhaps she truly did know.
I had disclosed nearly all of my research to her.
...If she was as mature as she seemed, it would take only a little thought to piece together the current situation.
"......If there's even a one percent chance of saving Satoko, I believe we should take it."

"That would be an extraordinarily dangerous gamble.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1025. ...In the worst case, she could immediately progress to terminal symptoms and lose all grip on reality, or else lose her consciousness entirely and spend the rest of her life staring blankly at a wall.
...Either outcome would be too cruel a fate for her."
"......So you're saying it would be better to let her die peacefully under general anesthesia, without feeling a thing?"
changeReason: Old translation simplified to 'die in her sleep' but Japanese specifies 全身麻酔で痛みを感じさせずに (under general anesthesia without feeling pain); new translation includes these significant medical details.

TextENGNew: "......Irie.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1018. As Takano always says, nothing in medicine is one hundred percent.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1019. But if we do nothing, the fate awaiting Satoko is one hundred percent certain death.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1020. So the choice should be obvious."
"......You're right.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1029. You're absolutely right."
But as the director, I understood better than anyone.
The experimental reagent C103 was still at a stage where we couldn't even say whether it was viable.
Its success rate, expressed as a percentage, was certainly less than one.
And I couldn't begin to imagine what kind of horrific end awaited her if the reagent didn't agree with her body.
That's why... I was tempted to believe it would be far more merciful to treat her to her favorite foods, watch her gently drift off to sleep, and then perform the dissection while she dreamed...
...As galling as it was to admit, Takano-san was right.
I was being indecisive—half-hearted, unable to commit.
...How effective could anything produced by such a man really be?
...Because I had so little faith in my own creation, I couldn't bring myself to believe in even a fraction of a percent chance of success...
"......I believe that if it's medicine you made, Irie, we can trust it."
changeReason: Old translation generalized to 'we can believe in it' but Japanese specifically says 入江の作った薬なら (if it's medicine you made, Irie); new translation preserves Rika's personal endorsement of Irie's work.

TextENGNew: Her words startled me, as though she had seen straight through to my heart.
Surprised, I raised my bowed head to look at her... and found an expression of such steadfast assurance that I could never have imagined it on someone her age.

"......Your work holds people's lives in the balance.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1023. It's almost presumptuous for a human being to try to govern life and death.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1024. The weight of that burden is something only those who bear it can understand."
changeReason: Old translation rendered おこがましい as 'absurd' but it means 'presumptuous/impudent'; new translation correctly says 'presumptuous,' capturing Rika's point about human hubris in controlling life and death.

TextENGNew: Her voice was the same, yet somehow I felt as though I was being counseled by a version of her I had never known...
changeReason: Old translation omitted 私の知らない彼女 (a version of her I didn't know) which is significant to the scene—Irie sensing Rika's hidden mature persona; new translation includes this.

TextENGNew: And she spoke as if she already knew, intimately, what it meant to carry the weight of human lives.

"...That weight is measured by the guilt and regret it adds.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1026. ...That's why you can't bring yourself to try an uncertain medicine on Satoko.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1027. Isn't that right?"
I felt as though Takano-san had said something similar when she criticized me before.
...And once again, I was being called to account for it...
"But...@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1029. That's exactly why I can entrust Satoko to you."

"Human lives are precious.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1031. The reason you buckle under that weight is precisely because you cherish human life.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1032. ......Without that compassion, how could you possibly cure anyone?
Do you think I'd trust medicine created by Takano, who sees people as nothing more than guinea pigs?@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1034. Hee hee hee."
"...Ha... hahahahaha..."
It was something I really shouldn't have laughed at, but I couldn't help it.
...It just felt right to laugh in that moment.
"If both you and Takano offered medicine,@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1036. I believe yours would be the one to heal her.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1037. Because medicine is born from the human heart...
changeReason: Old translation personalized the statement ('your medicine came from your heart') but Japanese states a universal principle 薬は人の心から生み出される (medicine is born from the human heart); new translation preserves this broader philosophical statement.

TextENGNew: ...The desire to save others, distilled from one's very being—that is what heals."
changeReason: Old translation heavily simplified the Japanese imagery of 自分の身から滴り落ちる雫 (drops falling from one's own body); new translation preserves this poetic metaphor about medicine being distilled from one's very being.

TextENGNew: "...You're absolutely right.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1034. ...To think a doctor would be lectured about medicine..."
"I offered my body for Satoko's sake, and no one else's.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1040. Every drop drawn from me by a needle is a gift I offer to Satoko.
changeReason: New translation includes 他でもない (for no one else/specifically for) emphasizing Rika's exclusive dedication of her body for Satoko; old translation omitted this qualifier.

TextENGNew: If you—someone who knows the true weight of life and never forgets his guilt and regret—created something from those drops,@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1042. there's no way it wouldn't work on Satoko."
changeReason: Old translation omitted 日々後悔と自責を忘れない (never forgetting one's daily regret and self-blame); new translation includes this, emphasizing that Irie's constant guilt is what makes his medicine trustworthy.

TextENGNew: ...Do shrine maidens hear confessions and counsel the troubled?
If so, she was already, at this tender age, a worthy maiden of Furude Shrine.
"......This will be a gamble.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1036. ...The question is whether Satoko-chan will accept it..."
"We don't need to tell her.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1044. Learning about Hinamizawa Syndrome would only tear open the deep wound in her heart all over again."
What she meant by those words...
...I didn't understand until later, and when I did, I was once again astonished by her depth of insight and empathy.

".........Let's cancel the dissection plan.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1038. We're switching to a C103 administration trial on Satoko Houjou.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1039. ...I'll do everything I can to raise the odds, even by a single percent."
"......Don't worry, Irie.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1044b. It will work."
"Hahahahaha...@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1043. You shouldn't be so optimistic."
"...It will be fine.@k@vS20/05/VTK1_rika1044d. It's already been decided.
That is... a most reassuring prophecy from the maiden of Furude Shrine.
......Go for it, yay☆"
